mission.title: kcp: flake: TestAPIExportAPIBindingsAccess
mission.description: flake: TestAPIExportAPIBindingsAccess. This issue affects 1+ users.

steps.title: Identify kcp feature symptoms
steps.description:
Check for the issue in your kcp deployment:
```bash
kubectl get pods -n cert-manager -l app=kcp
kubectl logs -l app.kubernetes.io/name=kcp -n cert-manager --tail=100 | grep -i error
```
Look for error: `Trace:	util.go:298`

steps.title: Check current resource configuration
steps.description:
Inspect the relevant kcp resources:
```bash
kubectl get resource -A
kubectl describe resource <name> -n <namespace>
```
```
--- FAIL: TestAPIExportAPIBindingsAccess (51.30s)
    kcp.go:130: shared kcp server will target configuration "/home/runner/work/kcp/kcp/.kcp/admin.kubeconfig"
    workspaces.go:191: Created organ

steps.title: Apply the fix for flake: TestAPIExportAPIBindingsAccess
steps.description:
Apply the configuration change to resolve the issue:
```yaml
--- FAIL: TestAPIExportAPIBindingsAccess (51.30s)
    kcp.go:130: shared kcp server will target configuration "/home/runner/work/kcp/kcp/.kcp/admin.kubeconfig"
    workspaces.go:191: Created organization workspace root:e2e-org-p2l7n
    workspaces.go:124: Created root:universal workspace root:e2e-org-p2l7n:workspace1
    workspaces.go:124: Created root:universal workspace root:e2e-org-p2l7n:workspace2
    virtualworkspace_test.go:359: root:e2e-org-p2l7n:workspace1: creating APIResourceSchema 1
    virtualworkspace_test.go:360: root:e2e-org-p2l7n:workspace1: creating APIExport 1
    virt
```

steps.title: Confirm flake: TestAPIExportAPIBindingsAccess is resolved
steps.description:
Verify the fix by checking that the original error no longer occurs:
```bash
kubectl logs -l app.kubernetes.io/name=kcp -n cert-manager --tail=50 --since=5m
kubectl get events -n cert-manager --sort-by='.lastTimestamp' | tail -10
```
Confirm that `Trace:	util.go:298` no longer appears in logs.

resolution.summary: This issue was resolved by applying the fix from the linked PR. The root cause was identified and addressed by the community.
